mechanism: Proposed mechanism: Bulk-forming agents swell in liquid, increasing stool bulk and promoting softer stool; effect starts 12–24 hours later; delayed absorption of co-administered medicines is consistent with mucilage/bulk effects.
safetyNotes: Contraindications include GI structural/functional disorders, swallowing difficulties, and undiagnosed rectal bleeding; inadequate fluid can cause obstruction/choking; discontinue and seek advice if abdominal pain or irregularity occurs; use not recommended under age 6.

sources.title: Community herbal monograph on Plantago ovata Forssk., semen (EMA)
Plantago ovata Forssk. - Plants of the World Online (Kew)

description: Medicinal material is dried ripe Plantago ovata seeds; powdered herbal substance is also a preparation form.
dosage: Adolescents/adults/elderly: 8–40 g daily in 2–3 single doses. Children 6–12 years: 4–25 g daily in 2–3 single doses.
interactions: Take at least 1/2 to 1 hour before or after other medicines due to delayed absorption.
preparation: Mix with sufficient liquid and swallow; maintain adequate fluid intake; avoid taking immediately before bedtime; avoid inhaling powder during preparation.
region: Native range SE Spain, North Africa to India and Somalia, and SW/S Central U.S.A. to N. Mexico.
sideEffects: potential choking if not taken with water
summary: Plantago ovata seed is a bulk-forming laxative for habitual constipation and for situations where easy defecation with soft stool is desirable. It must be taken with sufficient liquid; inadequate fluid can cause choking or GI obstruction.
